Category: Hospitality & Tourism: Hotels, Tourism, Travel Agencies, Food & Beverage
Who is this for: Food service managers, restaurant managers, hotel managers, kitchen staff, food safety officers, hospitality supervisors, catering managers, compliance officers, quality assurance staff
Team or Dept this is for: Food & Beverage, Operations, Quality Assurance, Compliance, Hospitality Management, Health & Safety
Description: Risks of relying on AI for hygiene and compliance checks.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with food safety principles and a general interest in how technology is used in hospitality settings are all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with relying on AI for hygiene and compliance checks in food and beverage environments, and explain how these limitations can impact safety standards in hospitality and tourism settings. You will also be able to apply practical strategies to critically evaluate AI-driven monitoring tools, ensuring they complement rather than replace essential human oversight in food safety programmes.
